title: carex norvegica subsp. inferalpina
intro: Carex norvegica subsp. inferalpina, also known as the Alpine Sedge, is a subspecies of plant in the genus Carex, characterized by its slender stems and flat, linear leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carex norvegica subsp. inferalpina include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Carex norvegica subsp. inferalpina is often used to make infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
